title: Don’t Ask Sean Penn for a Selfie — Even If You’re a Holocaust Survivor
content:
ByMarlow Stern

Chief Correspondent

Sean Pennhatesselfies and will not, under any circumstances, go to a public gathering with more than eight people — which is why he was noticeably absent from the recent 98thAcademy Awards, where he won Best Supporting Actor (his third Oscar).

The revelations came during a conversation between Penn and CNN anchorKaitlin Collinsduring a Friday afternoon talk at Spring Studios in lower Manhattan as part of the25thannual Tribeca Festival, running June 3-14. Penn is a regular fixture at the fest, owing in large part to his long-time personal and professional relationship with one of the fest’s co-founders, Robert De Niro, and was his usual frank and uncensored self during the talk, bouncing from topics like Ukraine (“they will prevail”) to his newfound passion for woodworking to why he’s mellowed out a lot since his hell-raising younger days.

But back to the selfies.

After complimenting his moderator, telling Collins she’s “brought so much of what real integrity is to covering this president” while noting that “if anybody ever had any question of who’d win the stare-down contest, I think this week was a killer,” Penn launched into an explanation as to why he wasn’t present at theOscars.

“It’s not just [that it’s] an awards show,” he said. “It would be the same if this group was going to an afterparty and one stepped into that. That always represented social discomfort for me; too many people. I’m now down, committed for life, that I won’t go anywhere to be in a designated group beyond eight people.”

The problem is that there large public gatherings only “gives you 15 minutes per person,” which to him triggers his “anxiety” and is “dread-provoking.”

“The two times I had gone I felt relief that I’d won because so many people out there had worked really hard for that,” he offered, adding, “There’s the politics of that stuff.”

According to Penn, he’d spoken with his “One Battle After Another” colleagues before the Oscars and they were all on the same page that it was “better for my mental health” if he not attend. So, hewent to Ukraine instead, where he’s been working tirelessly to shine a light on its being under siege by Russia, and how it’s in need of Western support. Penn still watched the Oscars in Ukraine, where it started at 2 a.m. and went until 5 a.m., and said he was grateful to not be there.

“I really got to enjoy the Academy Awards for the first time,” he admitted. “It was great.”

Penn made the decision to no longer attend awards shows after venturing to this year’s Golden Globes.

“The best that I could ever muster was relief,” he shared. “Knowing that I wasn’t going to do that anymore, I did one before that this year. I went to the Golden Globes; I’d never been to that before. And that’s where I decided, ‘I can’t do this.’”

“[It’s] the ninth person,” he continued. “And it’s not the low-hanging fruit of ‘I don’t wanna be around all this fake Hollywood’ or something.”

While he was leaving the Golden Globes, Penn was bombarded by people wanting selfies, which again, made him never, ever want to attend a public event with more than eight people.

“People should not do selfieseverwithanyone. It’s bad for you; it’s bad for everyone. It’s a soul-sucker,” he said. “It’s the Holocaust grandmother and her 6-year-old paraplegic wheeling over? It’s a hard no.”
title_zh: 不要向肖恩·潘(Sean Penn)要自拍——即使你是一位大屠殺倖存者
content_zh:
作者：馬洛·斯特恩(Marlow Stern)

首席記者

肖恩·潘(Sean Penn)厭惡自拍，並且在任何情況下都不會參加超過八人的公共聚會——這就是他在最近的第98屆奧斯卡金像獎上缺席的原因，該頒獎典禮上他贏得了最佳男配角(他的第三座奧斯卡獎)。

這些揭露是在潘與CNN主播凱特琳·柯林斯(Kaitlin Collins)的對話中披露的，該對話於周五下午在曼哈頓下城的春季影業(SPRING STUDIOS)舉行，作為第25屆特賣影展(Tribeca Festival)的一部分，該影展將於6月3日至14日舉行。潘是該影展的常客，這在很大程度上歸功於他與影展共同創辦人之一的羅伯特·德尼羅(Robert De Niro)之間長期的個人和專業關係。在談話中，他一如既往地直言不諱，從烏克蘭話題（“他們會勝利”）跳到他對木工的新熱情，再到他自年輕時代的叛逆行為以來為何變得更加平和。

但回到自拍的話題。

在讚美他的主持人時，潘告訴柯林斯，她“為報導這位總統帶來了真正的誠信”，並指出“如果有人對誰能贏得對視比賽有任何疑問，我想這一周的結果是毀滅性的。”隨後，潘開始解釋為何他未能出席奧斯卡。

“這不僅僅是[一場]頒獎典禮，”他說。“如果這群人要去參加一個派對，而其中一個人進去了，那也是一樣的。對我來說，這總是代表著社交上的不適；人太多了。我現在已經決定終身不會參加超過八人的指定聚會。”

他指出，這類大型公共聚會只會“給你每個人15分鐘的時間”，這讓他感到“焦慮”，並且“令人畏懼”。

“我去過的兩次，我感到的解脫是因為有那麼多人為此努力工作，”他表示，並補充道，“這裡面有政治的考量。”

根據潘的說法，他在奧斯卡之前與他的《一場又一場的戰鬥》(One Battle After Another)同事們交談過，他們都認為如果他不參加會“對我的心理健康更好”。因此，他選擇去了烏克蘭，在那裡他不懈努力，試圖引起人們對烏克蘭被俄羅斯圍攻的關注，以及它對西方支持的需求。潘仍然在烏克蘭觀看奧斯卡，該頒獎典禮從凌晨2點開始，直到早上5點，他表示很感激自己不在現場。

“我真的第一次享受了奧斯卡金像獎，”他承認。“這太棒了。”

潘在參加今年的金球獎(Golden Globes)後決定不再參加頒獎典禮。

“我所能感受到的最好情緒就是解脫，”他分享道。“知道我不會再這樣做，我在今年之前參加過一次。我去了金球獎；我之前從未參加過。那時我決定，‘我不能再這樣做了。’”

“[這是]第九個人，”他繼續說。“這並不是因為‘我不想和這些虛假的好萊塢人待在一起’之類的低級理由。”

在離開金球獎時，肖恩·潘(Sean Penn)被一群想要合影的人圍住，這再次讓他不想參加任何有超過八個人的公開活動。

“人們不應該和任何人拍合影。這對你不好；對每個人都不好。這是一種吸魂的行為，”他說。“這就像是大屠殺的祖母和她那個六歲的癱瘓孫子過來？這是個堅決的否定。”
summary_zh:
作者：馬洛·斯特恩(Marlow Stern)

首席記者

肖恩·潘(Sean Penn)厭惡自拍，並且在任何情況下都不會參加超過八人的公共聚會——這就是他在最近的第98屆奧斯卡金像獎上缺席的原因，該頒獎典禮上他贏得了最佳男配角(他的第三座奧斯卡獎)。
